reason: PROPERNESS ARGUMENT GAP: Lines 89-92 of lecA1.tex claim that because tau -> gamma^0(tau) is a diffeomorphism from I onto its image, it follows that gamma_j is a proper map. This inference is insufficiently justified. The argument must establish: (1) gamma^0 being a diffeomorphism bounds the tau-range of gamma^{-1}(K) (since K is compact, its time projection is bounded, so tau lies in a bounded interval) -- this step is implicit but correct; (2) the spatial coordinates gamma^i(tau) for i=1,2,3 are also bounded on this tau-interval -- this requires the timelike condition |dot-gamma^i| < |dot-gamma^0| to bound spatial growth relative to time growth, but this is never stated; (3) gamma^{-1}(K) is closed (uses embedding hypothesis: gamma is a homeomorphism onto its image, so gamma^{-1}(K cap Image(gamma)) is closed in I). The proof jumps from 'gamma^0 is a diffeomorphism' to 'gamma is proper' in a single sentence. At minimum, the boundedness of the spatial coordinates on the preimage should be argued. The fix is straightforward (the timelike condition immediately gives it), but the gap exists as written.
